description: Cressida Cowell's 2003 children's novel series, adapted by DreamWorks into a beloved animated trilogy (2010–2019) following Viking outcast Hiccup and his Night Fury dragon Toothless. One of animation's finest coming-of-age stories, with a live-action remake era now underway.

media.title: How to Train Your Dragon (Book 1)
media.author: Cressida Cowell
media.description: Young Hiccup must capture and train a dragon to pass his Viking initiation — his dragon turns out to be a tiny, toothless, talking runt named Toothless.
media.notes: Audiobook narrated by David Tennant (Hachette Audio). Very different from the films — dragons speak, Toothless is tiny and green.

media.title: How to Be a Pirate (Book 2)
media.author: Cressida Cowell
media.description: Hiccup and Toothless hunt for pirate treasure while battling a terrifying dragon.
media.notes: Audiobook narrated by David Tennant.

media.title: How to Speak Dragonese (Book 3)
media.author: Cressida Cowell
media.description: Hiccup must rescue himself and Fishlegs from Roman soldiers using his dragon-language skills.
media.notes: Audiobook narrated by David Tennant.

media.title: How to Cheat a Dragon's Curse (Book 4)
media.author: Cressida Cowell
media.description: Hiccup races against time to find a cure for a deadly dragon bite before Fishlegs dies.
media.notes: Audiobook narrated by David Tennant.

media.title: How to Twist a Dragon's Tale (Book 5)
media.author: Cressida Cowell
media.description: A volcano threatens Berk and Hiccup must prevent an Exterminator dragon uprising.
media.notes: Audiobook narrated by David Tennant.

media.title: A Hero's Guide to Deadly Dragons (Book 6)
media.author: Cressida Cowell
media.description: Hiccup must steal a priceless book from the fearsome Meathead Public Library.
media.notes: Audiobook narrated by David Tennant.

media.title: How to Ride a Dragon's Storm (Book 7)
media.author: Cressida Cowell
media.description: Hiccup must win a swimming race that takes him to discover America while battling Norbert the Nutjob.
media.notes: Audiobook narrated by David Tennant.

media.title: How to Break a Dragon's Heart (Book 8)
media.author: Cressida Cowell
media.description: Hiccup and Fishlegs are captured and must complete an impossible task for the Berserks.
media.notes: Audiobook narrated by David Tennant.

media.title: How to Steal a Dragon's Sword (Book 9)
media.author: Cressida Cowell
media.description: The Dragon Rebellion grows; Hiccup learns dark truths about the Viking king.
media.notes: Audiobook narrated by David Tennant. Series shifts darker from here.

media.title: How to Seize a Dragon's Jewel (Book 10)
media.author: Cressida Cowell
media.description: Hiccup is now a wanted outlaw hunted by Vikings and dragons alike as the rebellion escalates.
media.notes: Audiobook narrated by David Tennant.

media.title: How to Betray a Dragon's Hero (Book 11)
media.author: Cressida Cowell
media.description: Hiccup discovers Toothless's true nature as a King's Lost Thing while preparing for the final battle.
media.notes: Audiobook narrated by David Tennant.

media.title: How to Fight a Dragon's Fury (Book 12)
media.author: Cressida Cowell
media.description: The Doomsday of Yule arrives — the final battle for the future of dragonkind and Vikingdom.
media.notes: Audiobook narrated by David Tennant. Series finale.

media.title: How to Train Your Dragon (Film)
media.description: Viking outcast Hiccup befriends a wounded Night Fury dragon named Toothless, upending his village's war with dragons. The best DreamWorks film.
media.notes: Directed by Chris Sanders & Dean DeBlois. John Powell's score nominated for Academy Award. Streaming: Peacock/Prime. The entry point.

media.title: Legend of the Boneknapper Dragon
media.description: Short film: Gobber's obsession with a bone-armored dragon is finally explained in this 17-minute special.
media.notes: DVD/streaming short. Canon. Released alongside the first film's home video.

media.title: Book of Dragons
media.description: Short film: Hiccup, Astrid, Fishlegs, and Gobber reveal secrets of 14 dragon species from the Book of Dragons.
media.notes: 18-minute educational short. Good lore primer for new viewers.

media.title: Gift of the Night Fury
media.description: Short film: Holiday special where the dragons mysteriously fly away from Berk just before Snoggletog.
media.notes: 22 minutes. One of the better shorts — genuinely touching.

media.title: DreamWorks Dragons: Riders of Berk (Season 1)
media.description: Hiccup establishes the Dragon Training Academy at Berk while dealing with Outcasts and new dragon threats. Set after Film 1.
media.notes: Bridges Film 1 and Film 2. Original voice cast returns. Streaming: Peacock.

media.title: DreamWorks Dragons: Defenders of Berk (Season 2)
media.description: The Dragon Academy faces the Berserkers led by Dagur the Deranged, while Hiccup and Toothless face new mysteries.
media.notes: Season 2 of the Cartoon Network run. Streaming: Peacock.

media.title: Dawn of the Dragon Racers
media.description: Short film: A sheep hunt turns into Berk's first official Dragon Racing championship, bridging Defenders of Berk and Race to the Edge.
media.notes: 25 minutes. Explains the origins of dragon racing seen in Film 2.

media.title: How to Train Your Dragon 2
media.description: Five years later, Hiccup discovers a secret ice cave of dragons and must confront the warlord Drago Bludvist threatening the peace.
media.notes: Directed by Dean DeBlois. Significantly darker and longer than Film 1. Strong emotional gut-punch in Act 2.

media.title: How to Train Your Dragon (2010) Video Game
media.description: Tournament-based action game set after the film. Play as Hiccup or Astrid and train customized dragons for arena combat.
media.notes: Mixed reviews (Metacritic 58). Wii/DS emulatable via Dolphin/DeSmuME. Wii version playable via Dolphin.

media.title: Dragons: TapDragonDrop
media.description: Mobile physics game — tap and sling dragons at targets. Simple arcade-style tie-in.
media.notes: iOS only. Now delisted from App Store. Defunct.

media.title: Dragons: Wild Skies
media.description: Browser-based 3D flying game tied to the Riders/Defenders of Berk TV series. Find, train, and fly dragons.
media.notes: Defunct. CartoonNetwork.com game shut down 2019–2020 when CN series ended. Fan revival attempts exist. PC only.

media.title: Dragons: Race to the Edge (Seasons 1–2)
media.description: Hiccup and friends discover the Dragon Eye artifact and explore new lands and dragon species, set 3 years before Film 2.
media.notes: Moved to Netflix. Original voice cast. Seasons 1–2 of 6. Streaming: Peacock.

media.title: Dragons: Race to the Edge (Seasons 3–4)
media.description: The Dragon Hunters faction intensifies and the riders defend Dragon's Edge from escalating threats.
media.notes: Netflix seasons 3–4. Streaming: Peacock.

media.title: Dragons: Race to the Edge (Seasons 5–6)
media.description: Final Race to the Edge seasons — the series concludes its arc leading directly into the events of Film 2.
media.notes: Netflix seasons 5–6. Ends Feb 2018. Required viewing before Film 2. Streaming: Peacock.

media.title: School of Dragons
media.description: 3D educational MMORPG — raise and train dragons, attend Berk's academy, complete story quests alongside Hiccup and friends.
media.notes: Shut down June 30, 2023. PC/mobile. Considered semi-canon — included original Race to the Edge story content.

media.title: Dragons: Rise of Berk
media.description: Free-to-play mobile builder — construct Berk, hatch and collect hundreds of dragons, send Hiccup and Toothless on expeditions.
media.notes: Still active as of 2024. The most complete mobile HTTYD experience still playable. iOS/Android.

media.title: How to Train Your Dragon 2 (Video Game)
media.description: Film 2 tie-in: dragon flight school with tournament events, mini-games, and training exercises on Berk.
media.notes: Multi-platform. Wii/WiiU via Dolphin. Generally low-quality licensed game.

media.title: How to Train Your Dragon: The Hidden World (Video Game)
media.description: Film 3 tie-in action-adventure. New heroes Scribbler and Patch battle the villain Eir who controls dragons.
media.notes: Also known as Dragons: Dawn of New Riders. Switch emulatable via Yuzu/Ryujinx. Aimed at younger players.

media.title: Dragons: Titan Uprising
media.description: Mobile match-3 RPG — collect legendary dragons, breed them, and battle the Dragonroot Company.
media.notes: Still active. iOS/Android. Better production values than most HTTYD mobile games.

media.title: How to Train Your Dragon: The Hidden World
media.description: Hiccup discovers the Hidden World — a secret dragon haven — while facing dragon hunter Grimmel the Grisly. The bittersweet trilogy finale.
media.notes: Directed by Dean DeBlois. Concludes the animated trilogy. Streaming: Peacock.

media.title: How to Train Your Dragon: Homecoming
media.description: Holiday short film: Years after the Hidden World ending, Hiccup's children venture to find the dragons during Snoggletog.
media.notes: 22 minutes. Set after Film 3. Streaming: Peacock. A genuine tearjerker epilogue.

media.title: How to Train Your Dragon: Snoggletog Log
media.description: 30-minute Yule Log-style holiday special: a slow, relaxing window into the Haddock home during Snoggletog.
media.notes: Streaming special. Continuity inconsistencies throughout. More ambient viewing than narrative.

media.title: Dragons: Rescue Riders (Seasons 1–2)
media.description: Preschool-aimed spinoff: human twins Dak and Leyla were raised by dragons and lead a rescue team on Huttsgalor island.
media.notes: Not connected to the main cast. Dragons speak English. For young children. Streaming: Peacock.

media.title: Dragons: Rescue Riders — Hunt for the Golden Dragon
media.description: Rescue Riders movie special: the team must guard a precious golden dragon egg from pirates.
media.notes: Netflix special. ~45 min. Streaming: Peacock.

media.title: Dragons: Rescue Riders — Secrets of the Deep
media.description: Rescue Riders special: the team discovers ancient sea creatures beneath Huttsgalor.
media.notes: Netflix special. ~45 min. Streaming: Peacock.

media.title: Dragons: Rescue Riders — The Magic of Wonderglow
media.description: Rescue Riders holiday special involving Odinyule traditions and magical dragon abilities.
media.notes: Netflix special. ~45 min. Streaming: Peacock.

media.title: Dragons: Rescue Riders — Heroes of the Sky (Seasons 3–4)
media.description: Rescue Riders continues under the Heroes of the Sky rebrand on Peacock after moving from Netflix.
media.notes: Peacock exclusive from Season 3 onward. Preschool target audience.

media.title: Dragons: The Nine Realms (Seasons 1–6)
media.description: Set 1,300 years after The Hidden World in the 21st century — a group of kids discovers dragons still exist in underground realms.
media.notes: Loosest connection to the main franchise. Modern setting. Hulu/Peacock. Aimed at children 6–12.

media.title: How to Train Your Dragon (2025 — Live Action)
media.description: Live-action remake of the 2010 animated film. Mason Thames as Hiccup, Nico Parker as Astrid, Gerard Butler reprising Stoick.
media.notes: Directed by Dean DeBlois. Grossed $600M+ worldwide. Streaming: Peacock. Start of a new live-action trilogy.

media.title: Dragons: Riders of Berk Comics (Titan Comics)
media.author: Simon Furman / Iwan Nazif
media.description: Six-volume comic series (Dragon Down, Dangers of the Deep, The Ice Castle, The Stowaway, The Legend of Ragnarok, Underworld) set in the Cartoon Network series continuity.
media.notes: Titan Publishing Group. Out of print. 2016 also had two Defenders of Berk volumes.

media.title: Dragons: The Serpent's Heir (Dark Horse Comics)
media.author: Dean DeBlois / Richard Hamilton
media.description: Official graphic novel series set between Film 2 and Film 3, co-written by film director Dean DeBlois.
media.notes: Dark Horse Comics. 4 volumes: Serpent's Heir, Dragonfly, Burning Midnight, The Serpent's Heir Finale. Most canon-adjacent comics.

media.title: The Complete Book of Dragons
media.author: Cressida Cowell
media.description: Illustrated reference guide to all known dragon species in the book universe, framed as Hiccup's personal guidebook.
media.notes: Published as The Incomplete Book of Dragons in UK. Companion/lore book.

media.title: How to Train Your Dragon: A Journal for Heroes
media.author: Cressida Cowell
media.description: Activity journal / companion book in Hiccup's voice with prompts, dragon sketches, and training guides.
media.notes: More activity book than novel. Companion piece to the main series.

media.title: How to Train Your Dragon School (Spinoff Series, Book 1)
media.author: Cressida Cowell
media.description: New spinoff series set between A Hero's Guide to Deadly Dragons (Book 6) and How to Ride a Dragon's Storm (Book 7).
media.notes: Ongoing as of 2025. Directly in the book canon. First new Cowell HTTYD fiction in years.

media.title: How to Train Your Dragon Audiobooks (Books 1–12)
media.description: Full audio recordings of all 12 Cowell novels, narrated by David Tennant (who also voices Spitelout in the films). Tennant's performance is celebrated — distinct voices for every character including a lisping, stuttering Toothless.
media.notes: Hachette Audio. Available on Audible/library. Tennant's Toothless voice alone is worth the listen.

media.title: Dragons Adventure: World Explorer
media.description: Augmented reality mobile game exclusive to Nokia Lumia 2520 — explore real-world locations as dragon training grounds.
media.notes: Defunct. Windows Phone exclusive. Nokia Lumia hardware required. No longer accessible.

media.title: Dragon Training Legends
media.description: Mobile card-based mini-game app released as Film 2 tie-in material.
media.notes: Defunct. Delisted from stores.

media.title: How to Train Your Dragon 2: Official Storybook App
media.description: Interactive digital storybook retelling of Film 2 with animation, narration, and mini-activities.
media.notes: Defunct. Delisted. Children's app tie-in.

media.title: How to Train Your Dragon 2 (2027 — Live Action)
media.description: Announced live-action remake of Film 2. Cate Blanchett reprises Valka; Ólafur Darri Ólafsson as Drago, Phil Dunster as Eret.
media.notes: Announced April 2025 at CinemaCon. Release date June 11, 2027. Announced January 2026 cast additions.
